content_markdown:
The diagram below shows the pressure-volume changes for one cycle PQRS of a Carnot engine.

During which part of the cycle is thermal energy transferred to the surroundings?

diagram_metadata.description: A pressure-volume (P-V) graph showing a Carnot cycle with four stages: P to Q (isothermal expansion), Q to R (adiabatic expansion), R to S (isothermal compression), and S to P (adiabatic compression).

vibe_explanation: In a Carnot cycle, thermal energy is absorbed from the hot reservoir during the isothermal expansion ($P \rightarrow Q$) and thermal energy is rejected (transferred to the surroundings/cold reservoir) during the isothermal compression ($R \rightarrow S$). The stages $Q \rightarrow R$ and $S \rightarrow P$ are adiabatic, meaning no thermal energy is transferred.
